slides.title: Your cells run on a *fuel that fades*.
slides.sub: NAD+ is the molecule that keeps your cells repairing and producing energy. It quietly declines with age. Here is the science.

slides.title: NAD+ powers your cells
slides.body: NAD+ is a coenzyme every cell uses to make energy and run repair. Levels *fall steadily* as the decades pass.

slides.title: It runs your repair crew
slides.body: Sirtuins and DNA repair enzymes depend on NAD+ to work. Less of it is *linked to* slower repair and aging cells.

slides.title: Less fuel, tired mitochondria
slides.body: Mitochondria need NAD+ to turn food into energy. As it drops, *metabolic resilience* can fade and fatigue can creep in.

slides.title: Precursors rebuild the pool
slides.body: NMN and niacin are precursors the body uses to make NAD+. They *may help* restore levels and support healthier aging.

slides.title: Habits do the heavy lifting
slides.body: Sleep, exercise and fasting *can support* NAD+ naturally. Supplements work best layered on a strong daily routine.

slides.title: Give your cells a *better baseline*.
slides.body: Vibe Health scores the sleep, food, movement and steps that support your metabolism, turning your daily habits into one clear Health Score.
slides.cta_text: search ‘Vibe Health’ on the App Store

caption:
Your cells run on NAD+, and it quietly fades with age. That decline touches your energy, repair and metabolism. 🌿

NAD+ fuels sirtuins, DNA repair and your mitochondria, and precursors like NMN and niacin aim to rebuild it, while sleep and exercise do the heavy lifting. Save this. 🔖

youtube_title: NAD+ Decline: Why Your Cells Lose Their Fuel With Age
